title: Anemopaegma cupulatum
taxonomy: Anemopaegma cupulatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of the Americas. It is classified as a member of the genus Anemopaegma, which is part of the larger family Bignoniaceae. The Bignoniaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the bignonia family or the trumpet vine family. These plants are known for their showy, trumpet-shaped flowers.

intro: Anemopaegma cupulatum, also known as the "corkwood vine," is a tropical plant species in the Bignoniaceae family, native to the Amazonian region. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Anemopaegma cupulatum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory infections and other health conditions.
